items.title: Japan lowers travel advisory for parts of eastern Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Japan's Foreign Ministry has lowered its travel advisory for several parts of eastern Sabah following improvements in the security situation, according to a Japanese travel media report. Japan has reduced the advisory level for islands off eastern Sabah, excluding Banggi Island, Balambangan Island and several others, from Level 3, which advises against all travel, to Level 2, which advises against non-essential travel, Traicy reported, citing the ministry. The advisory for coastal areas from Kudat to Tawau, including Banggi and Balambangan islands, has also been lowered from Level 2 to Level 1, under which travellers are advised to exercise caution.

items.title: Anwar: Economic progress must reach all Malaysians equally
items.summary: MUAR: The country's development and economic progress must be enjoyed by all levels of society, especially those at the grassroots, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the national development agenda should not focus solely on large-scale projects or high-tech modernisation, but must also be felt by farmers, fishermen, padi planters and smallholders. "This development must be enjoyed by all and guarantee convenience to the people, especially those at the lower level.

items.title: Increase in special grant proves federal commitment to strengthen Sabah's rights - Armizan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The increase in Sabah's interim Special Grant allocation from RM600 million to RM1.5 billion reflects the Federal Government's commitment to strengthening the implementation the state's rights under the Malaysia Agreement 1963. Gabungan Rakyat Sabah secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, said the announcement made by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on May 31 marked a significant step towards strengthening Sabah's financial position. He said that although the case concerning Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement was still proceeding legally, GRS maintains its position that the review of the Special Grant under Articles 112C and 112D of the Federal Constitution must be implemented, including the gazettement of the revised grant this year.

items.title: Anwar to advance fuel supply agenda during Russia working visit
items.summary: MUAR: Ensuring the country's energy supply remains a key government priority, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim ahead of his working visit to Russia tomorrow for talks with President Vladimir Putin. Anwar said the visit, which will also involve a meeting between Asean leaders and the Russian leadership, would be used to secure the continuity and stability of oil and diesel supplies to Malaysia. He said the initiative was important to safeguard long-term energy security and avoid supply disruptions already affecting several neighbouring Asean countries.

items.title: Amanah Youth chief, deputy among nine candidates for Negri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Amanah Youth chief Mohd Hanis Alimin and his deputy are among nine candidates the party will field in the state election on Aug 1. Mohd Hanis said both of them had been selected to contest, although the party leadership would officially announce the candidates and constituencies soon. "God willing, I will be entrusted to contest one of the state seats within the Kuala Pilah parliamentary constituency," he said when contacted today.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Monday, June 15, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1464207]] [[nid:1464206]] [[nid:1464205]] [[nid:1464204]] [[nid:1464203]] [[nid:1464202]] [[nid:1464201]] [[nid:1464200]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Trump announces July 4 rally in Washington
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump announced plans on Monday to hold a major rally in Washington on July 4 for the United States' 250th anniversary, in addition to a concert event 10 days earlier. The president had previously said the June 24 event would be the "Greatest Rally, EVER!... But on Monday, Trump said the new July 4 event would be "the most spectacular TRUMP RALLY of them all."

items.title: Anwar welcomes US–Iran understanding to end hostilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed the understanding reached between the United States and Iran to end hostilities. "I welcome the understanding reached between the United States and Iran towards ending hostilities, and the announcement that a memorandum of understanding will be signed in the coming days. Anwar said Malaysia stood ready to support efforts towards a just and lasting peace and urged the international community to maintain momentum.

items.title: Island, helicopter and luxury cars up for auction at Insolvency carnival
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: An island, helicopter, yacht and luxury cars are among assets set to go under the hammer at the 2026 Insolvency Second Chance Policy Roadshow Carnival, which begins nationwide from June 26. Insolvency Department director Mohd Mukhzany Fariz Mohd Mokhtar said the auction was aimed at liquidating assets to help bankrupt individuals settle outstanding debts. He said e-commerce platform Shopee, along with several companies, would also conduct auctions of unclaimed items starting from as low as RM1.

items.title: Karak Expressway crash victims buried in single grave
items.summary: BENTONG: The remains of four family members killed in a crash involving a trailer on the Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway yesterday were laid to rest in a single grave today. The victims – A. Halim Hasoon, 55, his wife Rozita Buang, 58, their daughter Norbahyah A. Halim, 26, and one-year-old granddaughter Nayla Afya Aizat Faizal – were buried at the Felda Mempaga 1 Muslim cemetery here at 7.15pm. Earlier, their remains were taken to the Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Mosque in Temerloh for funeral prayers.

items.title: Foreigners not entitled to healthcare subsidies, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: He said non-citizens continued to be charged significantly higher fees compared with Malaysians. "Foreigners are indeed charged significantly higher treatment fees than locals. He said that foreigners were not subsidised by the government.

items.title: Norway crown princess's son gets four-year sentence for rape
items.summary: OSLO: An Oslo court on Monday sentenced Norwegian Crown Princess Mette-Marit's son to four years in prison on two counts of rape and 32 other offences, in a high-profile scandal that has rocked the monarchy. Marius Borg Hoiby, 29, Mette-Marit's son from a relationship prior to her 2001 marriage to Crown Prince Haakon, was accused of 40 charges, from rape to traffic violations, carrying a maximum possible sentence of 16 years in prison. Hoiby, in custody since February, had denied the most serious allegations, including the charges of rape, which the prosecution said took place while the women were asleep or unconscious.

items.title: Tanco: Business fundamentals remain unaffected amid share price volatility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tanco Holdings Bhd said its business fundamentals remain unaffected despite recent volatility in its share price that prompted Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd (Bursa Securities) to issue an Unusual Market Activity (UMA) query. In a statement today, the Main Market-listed company said all announced projects, including ongoing property developments, the Smart AI Container Port, and the Port Dickson Free Zone in Negeri Sembilan, remain intact. Meanwhile, Tanco said it had responded to the UMA query issued by Bursa Securities.

items.title: News@9: Today's top headlines - June 15, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: Anwar warns against division Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians not to fall for efforts to divide communities along racial lines. Driver tests positive The trailer driver involved in the fatal Karak Highway crash that claimed four lives has tested positive for drugs. ATM fees abolished Bank customers nationwide can make interbank ATM withdrawals without the RM1 fee starting July 1.

items.title: 11,000 people lost jobs in Selangor since January
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Eleven thousand people in Selangor have lost their jobs since January, says state Human Resources and Poverty Eradication Exco V Papparaidu. He said the figure was obtained from the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), which involved several employment sectors, especially the manufacturing sector. Papparaidu said among the ongoing efforts conducted in each district in the state is the Job Care Carnival, aimed at addressing unemployment.

items.title: Workshop owner, mechanic, cleaner nabbed in fake MC probe
items.summary: PEKAN: A workshop owner, mechanic and cleaner were arrested today for allegedly selling fake medical certificates bearing the name of a government medical officer. District police chief Superintendent Mohd Zaidi Mat Zin said the trio acted as middlemen, identifying customers and selling the fake certificates for between RM50 and RM200 each. "Investigations by the health office found that the document bore the name, stamp and signature of a medical officer.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds to hit 7 states tonight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are forecast to hit several districts across 7 states until 9pm tonight. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said in a Facebook post, the areas in Kedah expected to be affected are Sik and Baling, while in Perak, the areas are Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. In Kelantan, heavy rain and thunderstorms are expected to hit Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Jeli, Tanah Merah and Gua Musang.

items.title: Pentagon chief denies US facing munitions stockpile shortage
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth denied that the American military was facing a munitions stockpile shortage, saying on Sunday that it was a "manufactured story" peddled by the media. Hegseth's comments came hours before the United States and Iran said they reached a deal to end the Middle East war on all fronts, including Lebanon, and to reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz. Concerns over the Middle East war straining US weapons stockpiles deepened last month after Acting US Navy Secretary Hung Cao cited the conflict as a reason for pausing arms sales to Taiwan.

items.title: Fox strikes US$22bil deal for Roku to fuel streaming push
items.summary: NEW YORK: Fox Corp is buying Roku in a cash-and-stock deal valued at about US$22 billion in a bet that pairing its sports and news programming with a top TV streaming platform will strengthen its position as audiences shift online. The deal, announced on Monday, gives Fox access to the more than 100 million households using Roku's streaming platform, potentially helping the cable TV-reliant media company better target ads and reduce reliance on traditional distribution. Roku rose 2.6 per cent to US$147.50, but traded below the offer price of US$160 per share.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry mulls legal action over Haim Hilman's 'unfounded' claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is considering legal action over what it described as unfounded allegations by Jitra assemblyman Dr Haim Hilman Abdullah regarding the public university admissions system. In a statement today, the ministry said it was exploring appropriate legal avenues to protect its integrity, public universities and the country's higher education system from slander and baseless accusations. It said Haim Hilman had alleged that public universities were "selling places meant for our children to those with money" and that tens of thousands of students had entered public higher education institutions through a "back door".

items.title: ‘Looks competitive, but only one bidder fits’: MoF finds signs of government tender system being gamed
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) has detected irregularities in government tender procurement processes through the practice of tailoring tender specifications to favour specific vendors, said Treasury Secretary-General Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican. He stated that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also the Minister of Finance, has halted the practice of direct negotiations for government tender procurements. Johan said the government has passed the Government Procurement Bill 2025, which strengthens accountability and enforcement actions.

items.title: Pahang Sultan: Tahfiz students can become future AI, medical, and engineering experts
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 15 — Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah is confident that tahfiz education in the state can produce huffaz (Quran memorisers) who will become future experts and leaders in strategic fields. His Royal Highness said the inclusion of Pahang State Maahad Tahfiz and Pahang State Turath Maahad Tahfiz among the top three schools nationwide in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination reflected the success of the state’s tahfiz education system. My aspiration to make the Tahfiz Institute a leading tahfiz education institution in Malaysia has become a reality,” he said.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry weighs legal action after Haim Hilman alleges ‘backdoor’ entry into public universities
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) is considering legal action over what it described as unfounded allegations by Jitra assemblyman Dr Haim Hilman Abdullah concerning the public university admission system. In a statement today, the ministry said it was reviewing appropriate legal avenues to safeguard the integrity of MOHE, public universities and the country’s higher education system against slander and baseless accusations. It said Haim Hilman had made allegations, including claims that public universities were ‘selling places meant for our children to those with money’ and that tens of thousands of students had entered public higher education institutions through a ‘back door’.

items.title: Prosecution files appeal to reinstate death sentence for ex‑college student who murdered pregnant girlfriend, set body on fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The prosecution has filed a notice of appeal at the Federal Court to reinstate the death sentence of a former college student who pleaded guilty to murdering and burning his pregnant girlfriend three years ago. On May 14, the Court of Appeal commuted the death sentence to 40 years’ imprisonment and 12 strokes of the cane against Muhammad Fakrul Aiman Sajali, 23, after allowing his appeal. Lawyer Muhammad Nor Tamrin, who represents Muhammad Fakrul Aiman, confirmed the matter and informed that the notice of appeal was filed on May 25.

items.title: 6.2 magnitude quake strikes off southern Philippines
items.summary: MANILA: A 6.2 magnitude earthquake struck off the southern Philippines on Monday, the US Geological Survey said, a week after a stronger quake in the same region killed at least 65 people. The quake hit off the coast of Mindanao island at 5.18pm (0918 GMT) at a depth of 112 kilometres (69.5 miles), the USGS reported. The national seismology agency said no tsunami warning was issued.

items.title: Mohd Rizal Zubair steps down as Country Heights CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Country Heights Holdings Bhd's chief executive officer (CEO) Mohd Rizal Zubair has resigned to pursue other personal goals, effective today. Mohd Rizal was appointed CEO on April 15, 2026. "The group's business operations and ongoing projects continue to operate in the ordinary course of business and remain unaffected by the resignation.

items.title: Aluminium falls to lowest since late March on Iran deal
items.summary: LONDON: Aluminium prices fell to their lowest level in two-and-a-half months on Monday as a US-Iran framework agreement to end their war improved prospects for deliveries from Gulf producers, even though shippers were still cautious on transit through the Strait of Hormuz. The benchmark three-month aluminium on the London Metal Exchange was down 3.2 per cent at US$3,422.50 a metric tonne by 1120 GMT, having hit US$3,408, its lowest since March 30. LME zinc fell 0.2 per cent to US$3,577.50, lead rose 0.3 per cent to US$1,970.50, tin gained 2.4 per cent to US$55,025, while nickel added 0.9 per cent to US$17,980.

items.title: Dollar near 10-day low as US, Iran reach peace deal
items.summary: LONDON/HONG KONG: The US dollar hovered near a 10-day low against other major currencies on Monday as a preliminary agreement to end the war between the US and Iran sent oil prices tumbling and lifted risk sentiment. US and Iranian officials said on Sunday they had agreed on a framework for a deal to end their war, halt the US blockade of Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices slumped, with Brent crude futures down around five per cent to US$82.90 a barrel.

items.title: Mycron unit buys Shah Alam property from parent company Melewar for RM30mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mycron Steel Bhd's unit Melewar Steel Tube Sdn Bhd has proposed to buy an industrial property in Shah Alam from its parent company Melewar Industrial Group Bhd for RM30 million. Mycron said Melewar Steel Tube had entered into a conditional sale and purchase agreement with Melewar to buy a leasehold industrial land together with buildings located in Seksyen 15, Shah Alam. It currently serves as Melewar Steel Tube's corporate office and manufacturing facility.

items.title: Malaysia steps up preparedness ahead of strong El Niño
items.summary: The Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry said preparedness strategies and response measures were presented by relevant agencies at the first National Haze and Dry Weather Steering Committee meeting for 2026 held here today. It said the weather phenomenon was expected to bring reduced rainfall and hotter, drier conditions, with possible impacts on agriculture, reservoir levels, public health, and an increased risk of open burning and haze. It also said the Fire and Rescue Department would also strengthen logistics and manpower readiness for the Southwest Monsoon period.

items.title: Renewed hope for stranded seafarers near Hormuz after US-Iran deal: IMO chief
items.summary: LONDON: Secretary-General of the International Maritime Organisation (IMO) Arsenio Dominguez on Monday welcomed the peace agreement reached between the United States and Iran, saying it would help advance plans to evacuate thousands of seafarers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz, reported Xinhua. The IMO was working closely with member states and partners to implement the evacuation plan, Dominguez said in a statement provided to Xinhua. Dominguez said the agreement signalled a crucial return to peace, dialogue, multilateralism and diplomacy.

items.title: Kelat separated during musth, not due to abuse, says council
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kelat, one of three Malaysian Asian elephants at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan, was temporarily separated from the other elephants after entering musth, says the Taiping Municipal Council. He said Kelat displayed typical signs of musth, including increased activity and weight loss of about 220kg. He said Kelat had since completed his musth phase and had been reunited with the two elephants.

items.title: Semporna expects 50,000 visitors for Regatta Lepa festival
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The waters of Semporna will soon be filled with colourful flotillas of traditional Bajau lepa boats as the district prepares to host the 31st Regatta Lepa festival from June 20 to 28. Sabah Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said organisers were expecting up to 50,000 visitors for the week-long celebration of the Bajau community's rich maritime heritage, culture and traditions. "Regatta Lepa remains one of Sabah's most iconic cultural festivals, attracting thousands of local and international visitors each year.

items.title: Embraer sees demand for smaller jets in Asia Pacific
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Brazilian aircraft manufacturer Embraer sees strong growth potential in Asia Pacific as airlines look to expand services to smaller cities, replace ageing turboprop aircraft and improve route profitability. Embraer commercial aviation chief executive officer Arjan Meijer said the region remains a strategic market for the company, which already counts Virgin Australia, All Nippon Airways (ANA), Scoot and Bamboo Airways among its operators in the region. Meijer said Embraer sees opportunities in replacing ageing turboprops operating on regional routes in Asia Pacific.

items.title: Loke: Cabinet greenlights Malaysia Transport Safety Board, staffing talks underway
items.summary: SEPANG, June 15 — The Cabinet has approved the establishment of the Malaysia Transport Safety Board (MTSB), with the Transport Ministry expecting to table the legislation in the next Parliament session, Anthony Loke said today. “The establishment of the Malaysia Transport Safety Board (MTSB) has already received Cabinet approval. Separately, Loke said the ministry plans to expand its Safe School Zone initiative after a pilot programme involving 14 schools nationwide.

items.title: Malaysia to discuss in-car cameras for e-hailing drivers after assault cases rise
items.summary: SEPANG, June 15 — The Transport Ministry is weighing new safeguards to shield e-hailing drivers from passenger violence, even as it doubles down on a strict crackdown against transport firms that flout safety standards. Transport Minister Anthony Loke confirmed that the ministry will enter talks with e-hailing operators to bolster driver security. “Passenger safety matters, and driver safety matters as well.

items.title: Anthony Loke: LRT3 launch date to be announced in next two weeks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — The long-awaited Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) line is ready to begin operations by the end of this month, with Transport Minister Anthony Loke saying the official launch date will be announced within the next two weeks. Speaking to reporters after the 62nd Annual General Meeting of the Malaysian Road Safety Council (MKJR) today, Loke said trial operations for the line have gone smoothly and the system is prepared for passenger service. “Trial operations have gone well and the system is ready.

items.title: Teacher remanded seven days from today in Kuala Terengganu over suspected sexual assault of 13-year-old boy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — A male teacher in his 30s has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations into allegations of physical sexual assault involving a 13-year-old boy, police said. According to Kuala Terengganu district police chief Asst Comm Azli Mohd Noor, a report was lodged by the victim at 9.38am yesterday. In a related development, ACP Azli said police in Kuala Terengganu opened four investigation papers involving sexual offences against children between June 1 and 14.

items.title: Bukit Kiara longhouse redevelopment to give 98 families free homes, part of land to be gazetted as public open space, says FT minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said several key principles have been agreed upon by all parties involved in the Bukit Kiara longhouse resettlement and redevelopment project. Among the key terms, she said residents of the longhouse will receive replacement housing as previously promised, while development at the site will be strictly limited to 2.33 hectares. “The replacement housing will be constructed and completed first before residents are relocated.

items.title: No suspects yet in gruesome Tongod elephant killing as Sabah probe stalls over lack of evidence, says state minister
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 15 — Investigators have yet to identify any suspects in the gruesome killing of a Bornean elephant found decapitated in Tongod last month, with Sabah authorities saying the remote location of the crime and lack of evidence continue to hamper efforts to solve the case. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the investigation is still ongoing and authorities are waiting for it to be completed before drawing any conclusions. Jafry acknowledged the challenges faced by enforcement agencies, noting that Sabah's vast forested areas make wildlife monitoring a difficult task.

items.title: Selangor to roll out RM100 monthly aid for 50,000 families under Kita Selangor vouchers
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 15 — A total of 50,000 low-income families in Selangor will receive cash aid of RM100 a month for six months under the Kita Selangor Voucher initiative, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari announced today. He said the initiative, which will begin on June 30, is part of the RM140 million Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 1 unveiled on April 16 to help residents cope with global economic uncertainty, rising living costs and the impact of the global supply crisis. “Our target is for recipients to start receiving the RM100 monthly assistance from June 30, with payments continuing for six months,” he said after launching the package at the Selangor Economic Action Council (MTES) at the Selangor State Secretariat building here.

items.title: Govt allocates RM1m for Bukit Kiara longhouse resettlement upkeep, DBKL to manage maintenance, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The government has approved RM1 million for residents involved in the Bukit Kiara longhouse resettlement and redevelopment project, with Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) tasked to manage maintenance of the housing scheme, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar said the allocation is intended to ease the financial burden on residents after relocation and ensure the long-term upkeep of the housing area is properly managed. “Later, DBKL will manage and oversee the maintenance arrangements so that monthly costs can be covered and residents are not burdened,” he said in his speech at the ground-breaking ceremony for permanent housing for Bukit Kiara longhouse residents here today.

items.title: ‘We’re monitoring, not panicking’: Amirudin says Selangor still on track for RM2.8b target
items.summary: Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the state government did not see the need to take emergency measures because revenue collection remains on a positive track. State revenue collection reached RM1.6 billion as of May against this year’s RM2.8 billion target, with the current collection trend showing positive development. Amirudin added that Selangor also recorded a RM300 million surplus against total expenditure due to the state government’s savings.

items.title: Special state assembly sitting not linked to polls, says Selangor MB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The Selangor special state assembly sitting beginning Friday is not linked to the dissolution of the state assembly or any state election preparations, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said today. Instead, Amirudin said the three-day sitting on June 19, 22 and 23 will focus on the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 2, aimed at reinforcing the state’s economic and social support measures, reported Harian Metro today. He said this at a press conference after launching the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 1 at the Selangor State Economic Action Unit (MTES) meeting room in the state secretariat in Shah Alam today.

items.title: Rosmah lodges report over RM55m US property claim, police say viral account likely fake
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has lodged a police report over viral allegations linking her to the purchase or financing of a luxury property in New Jersey, United States, reportedly valued at RM55 million (US$13 million), Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. Tan Sri Mohd Khalid said preliminary investigations suggested the social media account behind the allegation was likely fake. “Preliminary investigations found that the social media account spreading the allegation is believed to be fake,” he said at a press conference during the launch of the Perangi Scam: Two Teams, One Goal campaign here.

items.title: Weather warning blankets nine states as MetMalaysia cautions of thunder, rain and strong winds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds for nine states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 6pm today. It said in a statement that, apart from the whole of Perlis, the adverse weather conditions are also expected to hit Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar and Pokok Sena); Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang and Dungun); and Pahang (Raub, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin). Similar weather conditions are also expected in Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak and Hulu Langat); Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin); and Johor (Segamat, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian and Kulai).

items.title: ‘Drop‑off’ deliveries now traffickers’ weapon of choice, police warn couriers to stay sharp
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 15 — Kelantan police have called in more than 10 courier service companies operating in the state to share information on the latest modus operandi employed by drug syndicates, which have been found using parcel delivery channels to facilitate their activities. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the initiative was intended to heighten vigilance among courier companies and their employees after numerous drug cases were found to involve courier services. He said courier firms had been asked to keep records of senders’ particulars, including details of their identity cards and phone numbers, to aid investigations should any offences arise.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan rules himself out of Johor race, says others should get the chance
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 15 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan has confirmed he will not contest in the upcoming Johor state election, despite speculation linking him to several state seats. Ahmad, who is also Deputy Works Minister, said he has no intention of contesting either the Benut or Pulai Sebatang state seat within the Pontian parliamentary seat. The Election Commission has set July 11 as polling day for the 16th Johor state election, with nomination day on June 27 and early voting on July 7.

items.title: Humanitarian approach towards Rohingya not a sign of weakness, says Perak MB
items.summary: IPOH, June 15 — Showing restraint when dealing with Rohingya refugees who violate local laws and regulations should not be mistaken for a sign of weakness, but rather as an expression of compassion and humanity, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said. Saarani said Rohingya refugees holding United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cards are subject to international law and should be treated appropriately while residing in Malaysia. He said the state government hoped the Rohingya community would understand and appreciate the humanitarian approach extended to them and refrain from violating local laws and regulations.

items.title: ‘We cannot manage what we do not know’: Saifuddin admits refugee data gap, pledges biometric fix
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 15 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has called on academics, civil society activists, think tanks, legal practitioners and security experts to submit constructive proposals on refugee management in Malaysia, an issue he described as a longstanding legacy challenge. Saifuddin stressed that the Madani government was committed to handling the matter responsibly, prudently and based on facts, with clear priorities — safeguarding national security while recognising humanitarian realities. We cannot protect national security without knowing precisely who is within our borders,” he said, adding that accurate data would enable more effective management of issues ranging from security and law enforcement to public health, employment and community welfare.

items.title: Chow: Penang Pakatan wants more women in the race but finding willing candidates still proves tricky
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 15 — Penang Pakatan Harapan (PH) hopes to field more women candidates in the next state election, although identifying a sufficient number of suitable and willing candidates remains a challenge, said its chairman Chow Kon Yeow. He said Penang PH remained committed to the target of achieving at least 30 per cent women’s participation in politics and decision-making, but continued to face difficulties finding enough suitable women willing to contest. In every election, we will field women candidates, but the actual number will depend on whether there are enough suitable and willing candidates.

items.title: Higher Education D-G says universities must deliver tangible impact, not just achievements that look good on paper
items.summary: Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said IPTAs today cannot focus solely on academic achievements. Rather, every ringgit allocated to these institutions must be translated into tangible impact on national development and the global community. “IPTAs must act as engines of national growth, centres of social reconciliation and dynamic problem-solvers for communities.

items.title: ‘Prepared to defend my innocence’: Tony Pua says likely to be charged over Rukun Negara post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Former Damansara MP Tony Pua said today he is likely to be charged in court over his Facebook post regarding the Rukun Negara, after police recorded an additional statement from him at Bukit Aman this morning. Pua said he was called in for further questioning after the investigation papers were returned by the Attorney General’s Chambers to Bukit Aman for additional questions. Pua said the additional questions mostly concerned the definition of the word “decree” and other matters linked to his Facebook post.

items.title: Banks scrap RM1 interbank ATM fee from July, giving Malaysians unlimited free withdrawals nationwide
items.summary: The initiative allows Malaysian debit cardholders to access over 14,000 ATMs and SRMs nationwide, regardless of their issuing bank, with unlimited free cash withdrawals, according to a joint statement from three financial services industry associations today. The statement was issued by the Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM), the Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (AIBIM), and the Association of Development Finance Institutions of Malaysia (ADFIM). “This initiative allows Malaysian debit cardholders to access over 14,000 ATMs and SRMs nationwide, regardless of their issuing bank, with unlimited free cash withdrawals,” they said, adding that the waiver applies to ATMs and SRMs operated by banks in Malaysia.

items.title: Police gear up for Johor, Negeri Sembilan elections, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Police are preparing to deploy additional personnel and resources to ensure the upcoming Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections proceed smoothly, Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said Johor alone is expected to require between 4,000 and 5,000 additional police personnel. “Johor is a large state with about 56 state seats being contested.

items.title: Reports of Kuching–Bangkok slots premature, AirBorneo says focus remains on intra‑Borneo recovery
items.summary: KUCHING, June 15 — AirBorneo has clarified that it has not submitted an application for a direct Kuching-Bangkok service and that no such route has been formally confirmed at this stage. In a statement issued to The Borneo Post , AirBorneo said while destinations across the region form part of its longer-term network planning, no application has been submitted for the recently mentioned Bangkok route. “However, we wish to clarify that no application has been submitted for the recently mentioned Bangkok route at this stage, as nothing has been formally confirmed,” the airline said.

items.title: Police bust mini casino, online gambling operation in Bukit Jalil luxury condo, arrest 15
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Police have uncovered a gambling syndicate operating a mini casino and online gambling activities from a luxury residential unit in Bukit Jalil following a raid on Saturday. Cheras deputy police chief Supt Lim Chun How said the district police headquarters’ Anti-Vice, Gambling and Secret Societies Division (D7) busted the operation during Op Noda/Dadu, reported Harian Metro . Police also seized various gambling equipment, including a Texas Hold’em casino table, hundreds of gambling chips, playing cards, RM4,904 in cash, laptops and a karaoke audio system.

items.title: Parliament set for fiery debate as Economy Minister tackles energy crunch and supply chain shocks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — The Ministry of Economy is set to deliver a ministerial statement addressing the global energy crisis and worldwide supply chain disruptions when Parliament reconvenes on June 22, Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir confirmed. He said the statement would include a debate session to allow MPs to discuss the government’s actions and plans in addressing the impact of the crisis on the national economy. Akmal Nasrullah said that the government, through the National Economic Action Council (MTEN), has increased its meeting frequency from six to 12 times a year due to pressures from the global crisis, with 124 decisions recorded so far.

items.title: Five Japanese men in Penang scam probe unable to enter plea as language hurdle delays court proceedings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Proceedings against five Japanese men linked to an international scam syndicate were delayed at the Magistrates’ Court in Bukit Mertajam today after language barriers prevented charges from being read out. The court also denied bail for all five accused. Earlier reports said police in Penang had dismantled three international scam syndicates in separate raids on call centre premises around Bukit Mertajam earlier this month.

items.title: Muda, PSM form Progressive Bloc for Johor and N. Sembilan state polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) and the Socialist Party of Malaysia (PSM) have agreed to form a political collaboration known as the Progressive Bloc ahead of the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections, according to a joint media statement. The statement said both parties had reached a consensus to work together throughout the election campaign period. According to the joint press release by Muda and PSM, the Progressive Bloc will focus on presenting progressive policy proposals to voters in both states.
